content:
First we place the points in the complex‐plane for convenience.  Let  
A=0,  
B=4,  
and choose  
C=6e^{iβ},  
with β>0 determined by |B–C|=5.  In fact one finds  
C=\tfrac{27}{8}+i\,\tfrac{15\sqrt7}8,  
so that |C|=6 and |C–4|=5.  

Next “triangle ACD” is similar to “triangle ABC” by the ratio k=AC/AB=6/4=3/2, carried by the rotation e^{iβ}.  Hence  
D–A =\tfrac32\,e^{iβ}(C–A)=\tfrac32·6\,e^{2iβ}=9e^{2iβ},  
and one checks |D–A|=9, |D–C|=\tfrac32·5=7.5.  In fact  
D=-\tfrac{423}{128}+i\,\tfrac{405\sqrt7}{128}.  

Now P lies on the ray DA beyond A, so in complex form  
P=-t\,D  (t>0).  
The condition ∠BDC=∠ACP  is equivalent to the single real‐equation  
arg\!\Bigl(\frac{B–D}{C–D}\Bigr)=arg\!\Bigl(\frac{A–C}{P–C}\Bigr),  
which one works out (by clearing denominators, conjugating, etc.) to force  
t=\tfrac49.  

Finally  
DP=|P–D|=|(–t–1)D|=(1+t)\,|D|=(1+\tfrac49)\cdot9=\tfrac{13}9\cdot9=13,  
so  
DP^2=13^2=\boxed{169}.

message.content:
First we place the points in the complex‐plane for convenience.  Let  
A=0,  
B=4,  
and choose  
C=6e^{iβ},  
with β>0 determined by |B–C|=5.  In fact one finds  
C=\tfrac{27}{8}+i\,\tfrac{15\sqrt7}8,  
so that |C|=6 and |C–4|=5.  

Next “triangle ACD” is similar to “triangle ABC” by the ratio k=AC/AB=6/4=3/2, carried by the rotation e^{iβ}.  Hence  
D–A =\tfrac32\,e^{iβ}(C–A)=\tfrac32·6\,e^{2iβ}=9e^{2iβ},  
and one checks |D–A|=9, |D–C|=\tfrac32·5=7.5.  In fact  
D=-\tfrac{423}{128}+i\,\tfrac{405\sqrt7}{128}.  

Now P lies on the ray DA beyond A, so in complex form  
P=-t\,D  (t>0).  
The condition ∠BDC=∠ACP  is equivalent to the single real‐equation  
arg\!\Bigl(\frac{B–D}{C–D}\Bigr)=arg\!\Bigl(\frac{A–C}{P–C}\Bigr),

t=\tfrac49.

DP=|P–D|=|(–t–1)D|=(1+t)\,|D|=(1+\tfrac49)\cdot9=\tfrac{13}9\cdot9=13,  
so  
DP^2=13^2=\boxed{169}.
